published_prior_art.cite: Manski1990NonparametricBounds and SusmannMcCleanDiaz2025NonOverlapATEBounds: bounded-outcome missing-cell/non-overlap width is driven by missing mass

structure_flags.label: Introduction
structure_flags.one_line: §4 does not follow the required four-paragraph order: paragraph 2 is main-claim prose, paragraph 3 is closest-prior-art/location, paragraph 4 is consumer, and the results-at-a-glance list is outside paragraph 4.

novelty_flags.label: Theorem 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The finite-tail width is the CausalSmith §3.1 Manski bounded-outcome missing-cell interval applied to a tail event.

novelty_flags.label: Theorem 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The endpoint formula is already the bounded-outcome missing-counterfactual argument in Manski1990NonparametricBounds, specialized to a tail cell.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The flagship gap cites KhanTamer2010IrregularIdentification and HeilerKazak2021ExtremePropensity without a section/equation/page anchor for the claimed sharp-width gap.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The stated consumer is generic: "limited-overlap inference and bounding designs" does not name a downstream paper, estimator, domain, or open question.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The stated consumer is generic: "ATT/CATT sensitivity reporting in observational studies" does not name a downstream paper, estimator, domain, or open question.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: With s1=1-e and 0<s1<=t<1/2, mu1(t)=E[e 1_tail] is at least nu1(t)=E[s1 1_tail], indeed mu1/nu1 >= (1-t)/t, so the alpha1>1 branch and A1(t)->0 branch are impossible under the proposal's own definitions.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The alpha=1 case ignores slowly varying residual factors; regular variation alone does not make A1 bounded away from both zero and infinity.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: The claimed open class with alpha1>=1 and finite one-sided CATT amplification depends on the same impossible/non-finite branch of the tail-profile definition.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: The displayed ATE formula says W_ATE=mu1/1+mu0/1 "up to" normalizations, which is not an exact mathematical statement of the frontier.

soundness_flags.label: Setup/Main results
soundness_flags.one_line: The proposal advertises a sharp identified set from observable P, but P^circ deliberately coarsens away positive-probability tail outcome regressions, so the kernel is a coarsened-experiment sensitivity object rather than the sharp PI set under P.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: After Theorem 1 gives W1=mu1/pi, the phase statement reduces to the ratio mu1/nu1 plus a routine regular-variation comparison, below the flagship novelty floor.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace the profile exponent with a denominator that can genuinely dominate target tail mass in some regimes, then add a non-corner exhibit showing all claimed phases are feasible.

Restate the object honestly as a coarsened-tail sensitivity experiment, or add restrictions explaining why positive-probability tail outcome regressions are not observed/usable despite being part of P.
Add location-level anchors for Khan-Tamer, Heiler-Kazak, Lee-Weidner, and Susmann-McClean-Diaz tying each comparator to the exact gap claimed in §4 and §8.

recommended_next_step: Reject this version and pivot or redefine the tail profile; the current alpha phase law is contradicted by mu1(t)/nu1(t) >= (1-t)/t under the proposal's own primitives.
